# Corpus v0.1 — junk-filtered + standing eval slices (Workstream E)

*Build snapshot 2026-07-21. Generated by `ml/corpus/report_v0_1.py`. v0.1 is a NEW pipeline stage layered on the frozen corpus-v0 shard — a drop-only document subset, so corpus v0 stays byte-for-byte reproducible ([corpus_v0.md](corpus_v0.md)). See [../../docs/phase-1.md](../../docs/phase-1.md) Workstream E and [../../docs/plan.md](../../docs/plan.md) §3.2/§5.3.*

## What changed from v0

Corpus v0.1 removes **3,508 documents** (2.44% of docs, 6.30% of o200k tokens) that match the audit's deterministic MT/CMS junk fingerprints. Every surviving document is **byte-identical** to its v0 form (provenance + content hashes carry over). Wikipedia bot-stubs stay **in**, flagged — their fate is fleet Q4's, not this stage's.

Reference tokenizer for token counts: `o200k_base` (same unit as v0 and Workstream C).

| corpus | docs | bytes | chars | ws words | o200k tokens | tok vs v0 |
|---|--:|--:|--:|--:|--:|--:|
| v0 | 143,940 | 497,397,899 | 484,340,940 | 91,129,087 | 151,638,173 | 100.00% |
| **v0.1** | 140,432 | 465,581,866 | 453,351,053 | 85,251,505 | 142,087,915 | 93.70% |
| removed | 3,508 | 31,816,033 | 30,989,887 | 5,877,582 | 9,550,258 | 6.30% |

## Junk filters (deterministic, first-trip attribution)

Each filter was **calibrated against the 200 human-verified audit labels** (the ground-truth set human-concurred in [audit_model_summary.md](audit_model_summary.md) §Human verification). The design target was **precision, not recall**: every rule fires on essentially zero human-labeled *natural* crawl docs.

| filter | docs removed | bytes | chars | ws words | o200k tokens |
|---|--:|--:|--:|--:|--:|
| `mt_placeholder` | 624 | 5,493,875 | 5,352,197 | 1,035,288 | 1,675,843 |
| `commercial_spam` | 702 | 7,556,446 | 7,345,527 | 1,406,619 | 2,225,530 |
| `price_listing` | 2,160 | 18,646,475 | 18,177,082 | 3,414,338 | 5,612,281 |
| `html_entity` | 19 | 109,765 | 107,056 | 19,888 | 34,113 |
| `foreign_script` | 3 | 9,472 | 8,025 | 1,449 | 2,491 |
| **total removed** | 3,508 | 31,816,033 | 30,989,887 | 5,877,582 | 9,550,258 |

Filter definitions (thresholds pinned in `corpus/config_v0_1.py`):

- **`mt_placeholder`** — ≥2 `XNUMX`/`XNMX` occurrences. A WordPress/Babylon number-obfuscation artifact; the literal string is never real Kreyòl/English.
- **`commercial_spam`** — ≥3 *distinct* gambling/crypto/pharma/SEO-catalog markers (distinct-marker count, so one incidental `bitcoin` in a news story does not trip it).
- **`price_listing`** — ≥4 price patterns (`$`/`€`/`£`/`USD`/`HTG`/`Gourdes` + digits): product / price-list pages.
- **`html_entity`** — HTML entities (`&#39;`, `&quot;`, …) at ≥2.0 per 1000 chars and ≥3 total. **Near-absent** in the MADLAD *clean* split (it ships pre-stripped; v0's `normalize` does not decode entities, yet almost none survive) — kept as a rule because it is the audit's canonical CMS fingerprint, but it removes very little here.
- **`foreign_script`** — ≥20% non-Latin alphabetic chars (Kreyòl is Latin-script): Thai/Chinese-dominant scraped spam. **Crawl-only** — Kreyòl Wikipedia legitimately carries foreign-script names in authored stubs about foreign places/people, which are not junk.

Evaluated and **dropped** as a rule: *untranslated-English-fragment density*. It fired on ~0 cleaned crawl docs and would false-positive on authored Kreyòl-Wikipedia bios of foreign figures (English/French proper-noun bodies) — documented, not applied.

### Calibration on the human labels

Running the applied filter set over the **151 crawl audit docs** (26 junk, 42 natural, 105 translated by the human-concurred labels):

| metric | value |
|---|--:|
| docs flagged | 6 |
| **natural false-positives** | **0 / 42** |
| junk caught | 2 / 26 |
| translationese-commercial caught | 5 / 105 |

### The honest finding: the 17% junk is mostly not fingerprintable

The machine audit put crawl **junk at ~17%** and crawl **translation-shaped at ~70%** ([audit_model_summary.md](audit_model_summary.md)). These deterministic filters remove far less than 17% because that junk is overwhelmingly **semantic** — fluent-looking machine-translated commercial text (product blurbs, SEO articles) with no mechanical tell. What *is* fingerprintable — `XNUMX` placeholders, multi-marker spam, price catalogs, foreign-script scrapes — is a small, high-precision slice. Cranking thresholds down to chase 17% would start deleting authored Kreyòl, which the calibration above is designed to prevent. So v0.1 keeps the residual translationese in the training corpus and instead measures it: the `translation_shaped_eval` slice makes it a standing quality axis, and whether to filter it harder is **fleet Q3's** decision (v0 vs v0.1 at fixed compute), not an assumption baked in here.

## Standing eval slices

Two held-out quality axes carved from v0.1 (seed 20260721), registered in `splits.yaml`, **never trained on**. Membership = a human-verified **tier A** core (the audit labels) + a conservative, documented **tier B** heuristic expansion. Like the tokenizer_eval holdout, they are excluded by consumers (training skips their doc_ids), not removed from the shard.

| slice | tier | docs | bytes | chars | ws words | o200k tokens |
|---|---|--:|--:|--:|--:|--:|
| `authored_eval` | A verified | 57 | 211,741 | 206,264 | 39,782 | 65,174 |
| | B heuristic | 300 | 681,032 | 665,228 | 115,484 | 213,507 |
| | **total** | **357** | 892,773 | 871,492 | 155,266 | **278,681** |
| `translation_shaped_eval` | A verified | 100 | 388,403 | 378,699 | 69,986 | 118,274 |
| | B heuristic | 300 | 2,194,563 | 2,139,898 | 411,272 | 662,292 |
| | **total** | **400** | 2,582,966 | 2,518,597 | 481,258 | **780,566** |

**Membership rules:**

- **`authored_eval`** (native-voice target) — tier A: audit docs the human concurred are `ht` / `ok` / `natural`. Tier B: non-bot-stub Wikipedia (`wiki_bot_stub=false`), seeded-sampled, capped at 300 (pool = 15,196 docs).
- **`translation_shaped_eval`** (contrast) — tier A: audit docs the human concurred are `translated` (all crawl). Tier B: crawl docs with a residual MT/CMS fingerprint too weak to be dropped as junk (≥1 HTML entity, or exactly one `XNUMX`), seeded-sampled, capped at 300 (pool = 627 docs).

Disjoint by construction (natural vs translated labels are mutually exclusive; tier-B authored is Wikipedia-only, tier-B translationese crawl-only). Probe proverbs are absent from the corpus, so they cannot enter either slice. **Intended use:** a model whose `authored_eval` BPB falls faster than its `translation_shaped_eval` BPB is acquiring native voice, not just web-Kreyòl — the Station 5 nutrition-label axis and fleet Q2's measure.

## Checkpoint prompt list — DRAFT (pending human sign-off)

The ~10 fixed prompts run at **every** Model-C checkpoint forever — the "watch it learn Kreyòl" slider (Station 6) and the demoable base→chat transition (Station 2). **This is a DRAFT for the human to sign off; it is not frozen and is used for no metric.** It is an *exhibit-design* choice, made once with care (changing it later means re-running every kept checkpoint). The full draft is `corpus/checkpoint_prompts_DRAFT.json`.

The proverb-completion slot is a **placeholder** — no probe-split proverb text is committed in any prompt set (Station-2 honesty: the model must not have trained on the proverb it is later tested on). The human fills it at freeze.

| # | category | prompt (draft) |
|--:|---|---|
| 1 | greeting | Bonjou! Kijan ou ye jodi a? |
| 2 | proverb completion (probe split) | <<PLACEHOLDER: first half of a human-selected PROBE-split proverb + ' …'>> |
| 3 | short translation (eng→hat) | Tradui an kreyòl: “Good morning, how are you?” |
| 4 | short translation (eng→hat) | An kreyòl, «I love my country very much» vle di: |
| 5 | question (factual) | Kisa kapital peyi Ayiti a ye? |
| 6 | question (causal) | Poukisa lapli tonbe? |
| 7 | continuation (narrative) | Te gen yon fwa, yon ti gason yo te rele Ti Jan. Chak maten, li |
| 8 | continuation (expository) | Ayiti se yon peyi ki gen yon istwa rich. Kilti li |
| 9 | register shift (formal) | Mesyedam, se yon gwo onè pou mwen prezante |
| 10 | register shift (casual) | Frè m, kite m di w yon bagay: |

## Reproduce

```bash
cd ml && uv sync
uv run python -m corpus.junk         # build corpus v0.1 (drop-only)
uv run python -m corpus.evalslices   # authored + translation-shaped slices
uv run python -m corpus.report_v0_1  # this report
```

Input shard: `corpus_v0-full.jsonl` (corpus v0). Seeds: eval slices 20260721. Filter thresholds + spam markers pinned in `corpus/config_v0_1.py`. Nothing under `ml/data/` is committed.
